requirements.requirement: Party Mode enforces configured token or cost budget for advisor and integration provider calls, exposes remaining budget state to orchestration, and emits thinktank.party_mode.budget_exceeded without raw content.

cases: [P0][5.5-BE-001][AC1,AC3] accounts provider usage and exposes remaining Party Mode budget in advisor and checkpoint metadata
[P0][5.5-BE-002][AC1] stops additional advisors when budget is exceeded and emits operational telemetry without raw content
[P0][5.5-BE-008][AC1] emits budget telemetry when the final advisor consumes the remaining budget
[P0][5.5-BE-009][AC1,AC3] integration action stops before provider calls when Party Mode budget is already exhausted
[P0][5.5-FE-001][AC1,AC2] renders advisor failure, omitted advisors, remaining budget, and recovery controls accessibly
[P0][5.5-FE-004][AC1,AC2] treats a persisted Party Mode failure assistant message as terminal and exposes failure metadata
Regression coverage for operational telemetry validation and raw-content rejection

heuristics.endpoint_coverage: Covered through existing session submit and stream paths; no new Party Mode endpoint was introduced. DTO validation covers new recovery actions and service tests cover provider-call guards.
heuristics.auth_authz_coverage: No new auth/authz rule is introduced. Existing tenant/session/latest decision validation remains the enforcement boundary and is exercised by Party Mode regressions.
heuristics.error_path_coverage: Budget exhausted before integration call, budget exhausted after final advisor usage, and mid-round budget stop paths are covered.

requirements.requirement: When an advisor times out or partially fails, prior Party Mode work is preserved, UI identifies failed/omitted advisors, recovery controls are available, and thinktank.party_mode.advisor_failed is emitted without raw content.

cases: [P0][5.5-BE-003][AC2,AC3] preserves prior advisor messages when a later advisor times out, emits advisor_failed, and exposes recovery decisions
[P0][5.5-BE-006][AC2,AC3] retry recovery resumes the failed and omitted advisor segment without replaying completed experts
[P0][5.5-BE-007][AC2,AC3] continue recovery resumes only omitted advisors and rejects non-retryable advisor retry
[P0][5.5-BE-006][AC2,AC3] allows Party Mode recovery decision actions through API validation
[P0][5.5-FE-001][AC1,AC2] renders advisor failure, omitted advisors, remaining budget, and recovery controls accessibly
[P0][5.5-FE-002][AC2,AC3] preserves prior Party Mode work and submits retry, continue, and return controls from latest failure message
[P0][5.5-FE-003][AC2,AC3] parses party_mode.advisor_failed before the terminal persisted failure message
[P0][5.5-FE-004][AC1,AC2] treats a persisted Party Mode failure assistant message as terminal and exposes failure metadata
[P0][5.5-FE-006][AC2,AC3] rejects advisor_failed without a persisted terminal failure message
Regression coverage for operational telemetry validation and raw-content rejection

heuristics.endpoint_coverage: Recovery still uses the existing message submit API and streaming parser. DTO and workspace tests cover retry-party-mode-advisor, continue-party-mode, and return-to-workflow payloads.
heuristics.auth_authz_coverage: Latest failure message binding and server-owned decision validation are covered; retry of non-retryable failures is rejected server-side.
heuristics.error_path_coverage: Timeout, retryable failure, non-retryable failure, malformed advisor_failed stream without terminal message, and omitted-advisor recovery queues are covered.

requirements.requirement: Automated tests remain deterministic with fake advisors and fake GLM responses for timeout, partial response, cost limit, successful integration, and single-advisor regression paths.
requirements.test_level: Backend service regressions + frontend component/parser regressions + TypeScript checks

cases: [P0][5.5-BE-001][AC1,AC3] accounts provider usage and exposes remaining Party Mode budget in advisor and checkpoint metadata
[P0][5.5-BE-003][AC2,AC3] preserves prior advisor messages when a later advisor times out, emits advisor_failed, and exposes recovery decisions
[P1][5.5-BE-004][AC3] deterministic fake provider success path remains unaffected and emits no resource failure telemetry
[P1][5.5-BE-005][AC3] single-advisor workflow calls still use the fake provider once and do not inherit Party Mode budget failures
[P0][5.5-BE-006][AC2,AC3] retry recovery resumes the failed and omitted advisor segment without replaying completed experts
[P0][5.5-BE-007][AC2,AC3] continue recovery resumes only omitted advisors and rejects non-retryable advisor retry
[P0][5.5-BE-009][AC1,AC3] integration action stops before provider calls when Party Mode budget is already exhausted
Story 5.3 serial Party Mode regression coverage for advisor order, current speaker events, and normal completion
Story 5.4 integration regression coverage for integrated conclusion and accept/return behavior
[P0][5.5-BE-006][AC2,AC3] allows Party Mode recovery decision actions through API validation
[P0][5.5-FE-002][AC2,AC3] preserves prior Party Mode work and submits retry, continue, and return controls from latest failure message
[P0][5.5-FE-003][AC2,AC3] parses party_mode.advisor_failed before the terminal persisted failure message
[P0][5.5-FE-006][AC2,AC3] rejects advisor_failed without a persisted terminal failure message

heuristics.endpoint_coverage: No live GLM/network/provider dependency is used. Fake provider usage, stream chunks, provider errors, and deterministic decision submissions exercise the relevant contracts.
heuristics.auth_authz_coverage: Recovery and return controls are bound to the latest server-owned assistant message rather than trusting browser-supplied Party Mode state.
heuristics.error_path_coverage: Timeout, non-retryable provider error, budget exhaustion, malformed stream, and single-advisor isolation are covered; full browser E2E is intentionally deferred because story scope is service/component deterministic coverage.

residual_risks.description: No full browser E2E was added for the recovery controls. This is non-blocking because Story 5.5 requirements are covered by deterministic backend service, frontend component, workspace, and parser tests without live provider calls.

recommendations.action: Keep the Story 5.5 resource ATDD, DTO, event service, ChatMessage, WorkspaceShell, and streaming parser suites in the Epic 5 regression set.
